qa_pairs.question: What are the distinct types of storage options available within our cloud environments?
qa_pairs.answer: Our cloud environments offer four primary storage types: Volume storage (the standard unit, attachable/detachable from an instance), Ephemeral/Disk storage (virtual local disk tied to a single instance's lifecycle), Object storage (non-hierarchical, for whole-file uploads), and Shared filesystem storage (private network attached, configurable on each mounted instance).

qa_pairs.question: Which specific storage type can be mounted on multiple virtual machines simultaneously and also provides automatic backups?
qa_pairs.answer: Shared filesystem storage is the type that can be mounted on multiple virtual machines (VMs) simultaneously, even across different projects, and it also includes automatic nightly backups to tape.

qa_pairs.question: Which storage type is automatically deleted when its associated virtual machine is removed, and what is its primary use?
qa_pairs.answer: Ephemeral/Disk storage is automatically deleted upon the deletion of its associated virtual machine. It is primarily suitable for temporary data because it is virtual local disk storage tied to the lifecycle of a single instance, and 'c' flavour local disks can be lost.
